statement.p: Line S has the equation y = ax + b and Line T has the equation y = cx + d\text{.} Suppose a\gt b\gt c\gt d\gt 0\text{.}

li.p: What can you say about Line S and Line T\text{,} given that a\gt c\text{?} Give as much information about Line S and Line T as possible.
What can you say about Line S and Line T\text{,} given that b\gt d\text{?} Give as much information about Line S and Line T as possible.

Since Line S has the equation y = ax + b and Line T has the equation y =cx + d\text{,} the variables a and c represent slopes. Since a \gt c \gt 0\text{,} we know that the slope of Line S is steeper than the slope of Line T\text{.} We also know that both slopes are positive.

Since Line S has the equation y = ax + b and Line T has the equation y =cx + d\text{,} the variables b and b represent y-intercepts. Since b \gt d \gt 0\text{,} we know that Line S and Line T both intersect the y-axis at positive y-intercepts and that Line S intersects the y-axis at a higher point than Line T\text{.}

webwork-reps.pg:
############################################# ### Generated from PreTeXt source ### ### https://pretextbook.org ### ############################################# ## DBsubject() ## DBchapter() ## DBsection() ## Level() ## KEYWORDS() ## TitleText1() ## EditionText1() ## AuthorText1() ## Section1(not reported) ## Problem1(1.3.16.213) ## Author() ## Institution() ## Language(en-US)
DOCUMENT();
############################################################ # Load Macros ############################################################ loadMacros( "PGstandard.pl", "PGML.pl", "AnswerFormatHelp.pl", "PGessaymacros.pl", "PGcourse.pl", ); COMMENT('Authored in PreTeXt');
############################################################ # Header ############################################################ TEXT(beginproblem());
############################################################ # PG Setup Code ############################################################ Context('Numeric');
############################################################ # Body ############################################################
BEGIN_PGML Line [`S`] has the equation [`y = ax + b`] and Line [`T`] has the equation [`y = cx + d\text{.}`] Suppose [`\newcommand{\gt}{>}a\gt b\gt c\gt d\gt 0\text{.}`]
a. What can you say about Line [`S`] and Line [`T\text{,}`] given that [`\newcommand{\gt}{>}a\gt c\text{?}`] Give as much information about Line [`S`] and Line [`T`] as possible.
[@ANS(essay_cmp()); essay_box(6,76)@]* [@essay_help()@]*
a. What can you say about Line [`S`] and Line [`T\text{,}`] given that [`\newcommand{\gt}{>}b\gt d\text{?}`] Give as much information about Line [`S`] and Line [`T`] as possible.
[@ANS(essay_cmp()); essay_box(6,76)@]* [@essay_help()@]*
END_PGML
############################################################ # Solution ############################################################
BEGIN_PGML_SOLUTION a. Since Line [`S`] has the equation [`y = ax + b`] and Line [`T`] has the equation [`y =cx + d\text{,}`] the variables [`a`] and [`c`] represent slopes. Since [`\newcommand{\gt}{>} a \gt c \gt 0\text{,}`] we know that the slope of Line [`S`] is steeper than the slope of Line [`T\text{.}`] We also know that both slopes are positive.
a. Since Line [`S`] has the equation [`y = ax + b`] and Line [`T`] has the equation [`y =cx + d\text{,}`] the variables [`b`] and [`b`] represent [`y`]-intercepts. Since [`\newcommand{\gt}{>}b \gt d \gt 0\text{,}`] we know that Line [`S`] and Line [`T`] both intersect the [`y`]-axis at positive [`y`]-intercepts and that Line [`S`] intersects the [`y`]-axis at a higher point than Line [`T\text{.}`]
END_PGML_SOLUTION
############################################################ # End Problem ############################################################
ENDDOCUMENT();
